educational_objective: Support naming feelings and faces with short stories and clear face books as opinions harden near two.
age_stage_nuance: At 22–24 months feelings arrive faster than words — colour maps, face pointing and short repair stories beat baby face boards alone.
what_to_look_for: Ages 1-5 / Ages 2+ / 2–3 years overlap, clear faces, short pages, UK bookshop or publisher primaries, max two brands.
what_to_avoid: Therapy workbooks, KS1 chapter length, bare Ages 3+ as sole mark, US hosts, Amazon primary, third Child's Play in Top 10.
methodology: Benchmarked uk.bookshop, Hachette and Little Tiger. Captured Ages 1-5 / Ages 2+ / 2–3y overlaps. Ranked by feelings-naming clarity, format fit and brand diversity. URL-verified 2026-07-24 Age marks captured from live listings.
buying_factor_memo: Order by feelings-naming clarity, face-pointing usefulness, short repair stories, ownership risk (borrow staples first), brand diversity max two.

top_picks.ownership_note: High duplication risk — check the shelf and library first.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A picture book where a little monster sorts big feelings into colours, so children can point and name what is going on.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Near two, feelings arrive faster than words. Colour sorting gives a shared label without a long talk. Borrow first if friends already own it, then buy if it becomes the nightly ask. Keep it nearby so they can choose it themselves.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #1: clearest feelings-naming metaphor for nearly two.

top_picks.ownership_note: High duplication risk — check the shelf and library first.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Bold faces and short lines cover many moods (silly, shy, proud) without turning bedtime into a therapy session. for sharing on the sofa at home.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Near two, moods swing hard. Todd Parr faces are easy to point at after a wobble when words lag. Distinct from Colour Monster because faces do the teaching, not colour jars. Keep it nearby so they can choose it themselves.

top_picks.ownership_note: High duplication risk — check the shelf and library first.
top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for heart shaped feelings read
top_picks.product_name: In My Heart: A Book of Feelings
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Heart shaped die cut pages walk through emotions with short, warm lines that suit lap reading near two. for sharing on the sofa at home.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Near two, big feelings need a gentle frame. Heart shaped pages make the talk tactile: shared looking, not a long explanation. Strong when Colour Monster is already on the shelf. Keep it nearby so they can choose it themselves. Share it together for a few minutes, then let them lead.

top_picks.ownership_note: High duplication risk — check the shelf and library first.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A short board book that names happy feelings with clear toddler faces and simple everyday scenes. for sharing on the sofa at home.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Near two, naming the good days helps too. One feeling per book keeps the page short after a park win. First Child's Play title; pair with the sad title only if you want a set. Keep it nearby so they can choose it themselves.

top_picks.ownership_note: High duplication risk — check the shelf and library first.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A matching board book that names sad feelings with clear faces parents can point to after a hard goodbye. for sharing on the sofa at home.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Near two, goodbye tears need a short shared page. Clear faces beat long stories when everyone is tired. Second Child's Play title; stop at two brands from this range. Keep it nearby so they can choose it themselves. Share it together for a few minutes, then let them lead.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #5: sad naming board; second Child's Play.

top_picks.ownership_note: High duplication risk — check the shelf and library first.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A bold picture book about a frog who feels grumpy, with humour that invites copycat faces on each page. for sharing on the sofa at home.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Near two, grumpy arrives as a full body weather system. A funny frog story names it without a scolding tone. Good when board feelings sets feel thin and you want a proper little story. Keep it nearby so they can choose it themselves.

top_picks.ownership_note: High duplication risk — check the shelf and library first.
top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for mine and share story
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A short picture book about two creatures arguing over a chair, with a soft ending about sharing and repair. for sharing on the sofa at home.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Near two, mine fights arrive daily. This story shows the squabble and the mend without a heavy moral. Useful when face books are covered and you need a turn taking tale. Keep it nearby so they can choose it themselves.

top_picks.ownership_note: High duplication risk — check the shelf and library first.
top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for one feeling per spread
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Each spread pairs a feeling word with a vivid face, easy to point at after a hard goodbye. for sharing on the sofa at home.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Near two, one feeling per spread is enough. Faces do the teaching while you keep the page short. Strong catalogue depth when Colour Monster is already loved. Keep it nearby so they can choose it themselves. Share it together for a few minutes, then let them lead.

top_picks.ownership_note: High duplication risk — check the shelf and library first.
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Monster masks and mood pages invite copycat faces: play first, label second, stop mid page if needed. for sharing on the sofa at home.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Near two, copycat faces beat long talks. Masks turn feelings into a game you can pause. Distinct format when standard board books already fill the shelf. Keep it nearby so they can choose it themselves. Share it together for a few minutes, then let them lead.

top_picks.ownership_note: High duplication risk — check the shelf and library first.
top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for shop floor meltdown story
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A shopping meltdown story that names anger and repair, familiar to any parent of a child near two. for sharing on the sofa at home.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Near two, shop floors invent new weather. A story that names mad and mend beats a long talk in the aisle. Check stock; some editions go thin. Keep it nearby so they can choose it themselves. Share it together for a few minutes, then let them lead.
